title: carduus adpressus subsp. novorossicus
intro: Carduus adpressus subsp. novorossicus is a subspecies of the Carduus adpressus plant, commonly known as the depressed thistle, which is a type of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Carduus adpressus subsp. novorossicus include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as supporting digestive health, due to its silymarin content. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
